summary: PR #2394 decompiled and improved progress for the melee lbmthp translation unit, adding bodies for multiple THP/movie-player functions, expanding THPDecComp fields and THP decoder prototypes, adding required includes, replacing several global-object references with Movieplayer field access, and using assert/report/cache/devcom/texture APIs. Bot report records 11 unmatched-item improvements in main/melee/lb/lbmthp, including large gains for fn_8001ECF4, fn_8001E910, lbMthp8001FAA0, fn_8001F13C, lbMthp_8001F410, fn_8001F2A4, lbMthp_8001F67C, and fn_8001EF5C.

key_files.role: Main decompilation target; replaces placeholder comments with C bodies for THP streaming, decode, alarm/frame update, texture load, teardown, and single-frame decode routines; adds includes and API usage.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: placeholders #fn_8001E910, #fn_8001ECF4, #fn_8001EF5C, #fn_8001F13C, #fn_8001F2A4, #lbMthp_8001F410, #lbMthp_8001F67C, #lbMthp8001FAA0 replaced with function bodies
text_corpus: bot report lists 11 improvements for main/melee/lb/lbmthp

key_files.role: Public/header surface updated with THP decoder declarations and expanded THPDecComp field layout used by new C bodies.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: adds THPDec_8032F8D4, THPDec_80331340, THPDec_803313D0, THPVideoDecode declarations
raw_diff: replaces padding in THPDecComp with fields including unk_18, unk_20, unk_28, unk_4C, unk_50, unk_54, unk_58, unk_64, unk_74, unk_8C, unk_98, unk_120, currPackedSize

key_files.role: Static/internal lbmthp declarations or layout adjusted for the PR; exact diff content was not loaded beyond changed-file metadata.

what_changed.text: Added new includes needed by the reconstructed lbmthp bodies, including Runtime/runtime.h, MSL/string.h, OSCache.h, baselib debug/devcom headers.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: include block additions in src/melee/lb/lbmthp.c

what_changed.text: Implemented callback/streaming logic in fn_8001E910 using OSGetTick timing, Movieplayer ring-buffer fields, HSD_ASSERT/HSD_ASSERTREPORT, HSD_DevComRequest, and OS interrupt guards.

Implemented buffer setup and initial packed-frame loading in fn_8001ECF4, including Y/UV buffer placement, DCInvalidateRange calls, lbFile_800161C4 requests, OSReport diagnostics, and an HSD_ASSERT failure path.
Implemented frame decode path in fn_8001EF5C using THPVideoDecode, THPDec_80331340/THPDec_803313D0, cache stores for 0x280-wide output, and interrupt-protected frame index updates.
Implemented stream prefetch/update scheduling in fn_8001F13C with HSD_DevComRequest and ALIGN_32 for currPackedSize.
Implemented frame-rate-table handling in fn_8001F2A4 using repeated loops over count/ticks pairs to convert ticks to frame index and trigger fn_8001F06C on frame changes.
Implemented lbMthp_8001F410 startup/allocation/alarm setup and lbMthp_8001F67C GX texture upload path for Y, U, and V planes.
Cleaned several existing functions from lbl_804333E0 field access or raw offset casts to Movieplayer field access, including power, unk_70, unk_140, unk_150, and status accessors.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: lbMthp_8001F578, lbMthp_8001F5C4, lbMthp_8001F5D4, lbMthp_8001F5E4, lbMthp_8001F5F4, lbMthp_8001F604, lbMthp_8001F614, lbMthp_8001F800, fn_8001F294, lbMthp_8001F87C changes

what_changed.text: Implemented lbMthp8001FAA0 one-shot THP decode setup using width/height header, THPInit, file load, YUV allocation/cache invalidation, THP decoder setup/decode, and cleanup.
Updated lbmthp.h with additional THP decoder prototypes and expanded THPDecComp layout fields needed by the new bodies.

smart_moves.text: Recovered struct fields in THPDecComp before using them from the new C bodies, reducing raw offset access in the implementation.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: THPDecComp padding replaced with typed fields
raw_diff: lbmthp.c uses data->unk_4C, data->unk_50, data->unk_54, data->unk_58, data->currPackedSize

smart_moves.text: Used existing project/platform APIs and macros rather than open-coded equivalents where evident: HSD_ASSERT, HSD_ASSERTREPORT, ALIGN_32, OSDisableInterrupts/OSRestoreInterrupts, DCInvalidateRange/DCStoreRange, GXInitTexObj, GXLoadTexObj.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_8001E910, fn_8001ECF4, fn_8001EF5C, fn_8001F13C, lbMthp_8001F67C

smart_moves.text: Converted several generated-looking global pointer/offset accesses to direct Movieplayer field accesses, improving reviewability when the global object and fields were available.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: lbMthp_8001F800 changes from base/p_14C and offset casts to Movieplayer.power, Movieplayer.unk_70, Movieplayer.unk_140, Movieplayer.unk_150

smart_moves.text: Kept loop forms for repeated frame-rate-table conversion and frame-buffer setup rather than leaving those sections as copied repeated blocks.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_8001ECF4 for loop over data->unk_104

decomp_lessons.lesson: For lbmthp-style code, recovering the component struct layout can unlock cleaner implementation bodies: once buffer pointers, frame counters, packed-size fields, and power/state fields are represented in THPDecComp/Movieplayer, many raw offset or label-based accesses can become direct field access.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: THPDecComp fields added in lbmthp.h
raw_diff: lbMthp_8001F800 and accessors changed to Movieplayer fields

decomp_lessons.lesson: When reconstructing async streaming/decode code, preserve narrow interrupt windows around shared playback state updates and decoder calls if the function evidence shows OSDisableInterrupts/OSRestoreInterrupts bracketing those operations.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_8001E910 interrupt block around stream request state
raw_diff: fn_8001EF5C interrupt blocks around THPVideoDecode and unk_94 update
raw_diff: fn_8001F13C starts with OSDisableInterrupts and returns OSRestoreInterrupts(intr)

decomp_lessons.lesson: Use project assert/report macros for recovered diagnostic blocks when the diff evidence includes file-line-style assertions and report strings, rather than preserving raw assert/report expansion.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: HSD_ASSERTREPORT at 0x121 with filnum/ofs diagnostic

naming_conventions.text: Global playback object references are normalized to Movieplayer in touched code instead of lbl_804333E0 where the field role is represented.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: multiple replacements from lbl_804333E0.* to Movieplayer.*

naming_conventions.text: Recovered known semantic names are used sparingly: currPackedSize, width, height, power, memoryRequired, rate_table, streamPlayer, y_size, uv_size; many unknown fields remain unk_* rather than guessed.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: THPDecComp contains currPackedSize, width, height
raw_diff: lbMthp_8001F410 local names memoryRequired and rate_table

naming_conventions.text: Address-style function names are retained for unrecovered function semantics, e.g. fn_8001E910, fn_8001ECF4, fn_8001EF5C, fn_8001F13C, fn_8001F2A4.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: implemented functions retain fn_8001* names

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: PAD_STACK was introduced in fn_8001EBF0 and fn_8001ECF4 to affect stack layout.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: The PR slice does not include objdiff/build details proving the PAD_STACK necessity beyond bot progress report; keep as a narrow tactic requiring verification.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used ALIGN_32 or equivalent packed-size rounding for devcom/file requests: (size + 0x1F) & 0xFFFFFFE0 and ALIGN_32(currPackedSize).
Used a small local struct for the THP header in lbMthp8001FAA0, then memset and field assignment before THPVideoDecode.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: lbMthp8001FAA0 local struct with u16 w, u16 h, pad[0x18], memset(&header, 0, 0x1CU)

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Repeated rate-table conversion was expressed as loops over count/ticks pairs rather than duplicated hand-coded arithmetic only.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_8001F2A4 loops using rate_ptr += 2, count, ticks, total

review_feedback.text: decomp-dev[bot] reported 11 improvements in unmatched items for GALE01 a179940 - e61e93f, all in main/melee/lb/lbmthp.

Reported improvements included fn_8001ECF4 to 94.05%, fn_8001E910 to 81.22%, .data to 91.42%, lbMthp8001FAA0 to 99.74%, fn_8001F13C to 99.98%, lbMthp_8001F410 to 95.41%, fn_8001F2A4 to 90.79%, lbMthp_8001F67C to 83.79%, fn_8001EF5C to 99.85%, .sdata2 to 83.33%, and fn_8001EBF0 from 98.63% to 98.77%.
No human review comments were loaded in this PR slice.

evidence_refs: metadata_json: PR metadata, changed file list, merge timestamps, counts
human_pr_text: body only says Tracking PR as usual
raw_diff: large but truncated diff excerpt with main lbmthp.c and partial lbmthp.h changes
changed_files.jsonl: all three changed files and line counts
notes: Strong evidence for changed file list, PR identity, author, merge timestamp, and many concrete code changes in lbmthp.c/lbmthp.h.
No human review comments were loaded, so reviewer intent and requested changes cannot be inferred.
The raw diff is truncated, and lbmthp.static.h content is not visible beyond changed-file metadata; avoid detailed claims about that file.
Bot report provides progress percentages but not full build/objdiff logs or per-tactic verification, so PAD_STACK and near-match claims should remain evidence-qualified.
The PR body is minimal and does not explain author intent.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Recover lbmthp component fields before cleaning playback code
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2394, expanding THPDecComp fields and using Movieplayer field access made the lbmthp implementations more reviewable than raw label/offset access, while retaining unknown names where semantics were not proven.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: lbl_804333E0 access replaced with Movieplayer field access

accepted_candidate_records.title: Keep interrupt guards visible in recovered async playback routines
accepted_candidate_records.text: The recovered lbmthp streaming/decode routines in PR #2394 show OSDisableInterrupts/OSRestoreInterrupts bracketing shared Movieplayer state updates and decode/request operations; similar async movie-player work should preserve those critical sections when supported by local evidence.

source_update_candidates.title: src/melee/lb/lbmthp handles THP movie playback/decode and texture upload
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2394 evidence shows src/melee/lb/lbmthp.c contains THP movie playback and decode logic, including THPVideoDecode/THPDec calls, file/devcom streaming, Movieplayer state, YUV buffer management, GX texture object setup, and alarm-driven frame advancement.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: lbMthp8001FAA0 THPInit and one-shot decode
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful path classification candidate for future searches; route to path_facts curator because intake should not promote source-owned facts.

source_update_candidates.title: PAD_STACK in lbmthp should carry narrow verification
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2394 introduces PAD_STACK in fn_8001EBF0 and fn_8001ECF4 while the bot report shows progress but no loaded per-function objdiff proof for the stack tactic. This reinforces the existing standard that PAD_STACK requires explicit narrow verification and should not become normal style.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot progress report for lbmthp
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential reinforcing example for existing matching-tactics guidance; not a new accepted rule from this slice alone.

rejection_notes.text: Do not infer exact matches from the bot report percentages; several functions are near but not necessarily 100%, and no full objdiff logs were loaded.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report lists percentages such as 94.05%, 81.22%, 99.74%, 99.98%, 99.85%

rejection_notes.text: Do not promote any detailed lesson about lbmthp.static.h from this intake; the file is listed as changed but its diff content is not loaded.
Do not infer reviewer approval rationale or requested changes; no human review comments were loaded.
Do not treat remaining unk_* field names as semantic knowledge; the PR mostly keeps unknown fields unknown.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: THPDecComp and Movieplayer usage retain many unk_* names
